doctrine-check.reason: Applied the enshrined rule (tier-system.md §2, owner directive 2026-05-25 't2>t3 this is enshrined') that systematic reviews / meta-analyses sit at T2. The re-tier corrects rows the 2026-05-25 canonicalization's WHERE clause (evidence_type='sr_meta') could not reach because their evidence_type was mis-set to clinical/grey. Doctrinally-unresolved cases (scoping reviews, one rapid review, one narrative review) were held at T3 and logged as GAP-298 rather than decided unilaterally.

evidence-verification-gate.reason: Each re-tiered row was confirmed to be a genuine systematic review via a real retrieval call before/after migration: REF-00398 WebFetch (mdpi.com, PRISMA); REF-00097 PMID 36082841; REF-00240 PMID 28990665; REF-00642 and REF-00570 WebSearch (publisher pages, database+study counts); REF-00227 PMID 33421589; REF-00607 PMID 39246212; REF-00638 PMID 37947544; REF-00639 PMID 35996349; REF-00611/00637/00256 confirmed by the independent refuter. No row was re-tiered on stored metadata alone without a self-declared SR/meta title.

source-discipline.reason: The anti-fabrication / mislabel gate fired: a fresh-context adversarial refuter re-retrieved the rows not independently checked in the first pass and caught REF-00589 as a narrative literature review (PubMed PMID 35695710, article_types ['Journal Article','Review']) mislabeled 'systematic review.' in the DB. It was reverted T2->T3 and its title corrected in a forward-only compensating migration; the original migration was not edited.

bias_direction: The temptation here is anchor-inflation: re-tiering borderline reviews upward makes weak slices look better-evidenced and lifts the corpus T2 count, which flatters the audit. Bounded by tiering strictly on the source's own self-declared methodology, verifying against external indexes rather than stored metadata, holding every ambiguous review-species (scoping, rapid, narrative) at T3 pending an owner decision, and running an adversarial pass that actively removed one row rather than adding any.
independent_reviewer_counterclaim: A reviewer could argue that applying the 2026-05-25 directive to thirteen rows it did not name is editorial extension beyond the enshrined migration, not the directive itself. Response: the directive enshrines a rule about a source class, not a hand-picked row list; these rows are members of that class stranded only by a mis-set evidence_type, and the doctrinally-unresolved members were explicitly withheld under GAP-298 rather than swept in.

reattestation.reason: sr_meta->T2 re-tiering, consistent with core-reconcile's 'sr_meta at T2'. The weak-band delta concerns T4-6, not sr_meta. Verdict unchanged.
